design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore non-linear labyrinth in humanoid form to locate and enter dungeon corridors; transform into spaceship to battle through shoot 'em up sections and defeat bosses; destroy 10 safety devices to activate self-destruct sequence before alien world Naju reaches Earth
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (power chips for weapons and upgrades vs. currency for shops) combined with navigation puzzle-solving (finding keys to unlock warp panels to access different labyrinth areas) and combat execution in both exploration and dungeon phases
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Solo guardian tasked with infiltrating an alien world as humanity's last hope; transformation between humanoid explorer and spaceship combatant; gradual power escalation through collected upgrades
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid genre design (Zelda-like exploration + 1942-style shoot 'em ups) with asymmetric gameplay phases; non-linear progression gated by key-based warp panel system; password checkpointing system tied to NPC Blue Landers rather than traditional save points; power chips serve dual purpose as both consumable ammo currency and shop currency, creating meaningful resource trade-offs

difficulty.lives_system: Single life bar (life meter) that depletes on damage; meter replenishes via collectible items found in labyrinth and dropped by defeated enemies
difficulty.continues_system: Game ends when life meter reaches zero; Blue Lander NPCs function as checkpoints allowing restart in designated rooms (only if system has not been powered off)
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-based via Blue Lander rooms; players can resume at checkpoint location with progress retained, but password system required for persistent saves across power cycles
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can control difficulty indirectly through upgrade purchasing decisions (allocating power chips between weapon improvements vs. capacity increases); non-linear progression allows choosing which dungeons/bosses to attempt in varying orders; ability to grind for power chips affects readiness for encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Power chips collected from enemies and bosses serve as currency; shops throughout Naju sell: primary weapon improvements, secondary weapons, and Landers (Blue Landers increase max health; Red Landers increase max power chips). Upgrades persist across checkpoint resets.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Warp panel keys found in labyrinth unlock access to different colored portal gates, enabling non-linear exploration; some dungeon corridors sealed and require specific in-game actions or clues to unseal; ten mandatory safety device corridors must be located and cleared to complete game
replayability_hooks: Non-linear exploration order allows different boss sequences
Password system encourages multiple playthroughs with different upgrade configurations
Bonus mode accessible via password 'TGL' (NA/EU) or 'えいえるじい' (JP) that cycles through all scrolling shooter stages
Labyrinth randomizer utility exists (mentioned in Data Crystal)

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: five hostile alien tribes vying for territorial control within Naju visible through enemy encounters
NPC dialogue via Blue Lander NPCs that dispense advice and story clues
Message panels scattered throughout labyrinth containing text from predecessor guardian(s) who attempted self-destruct mechanism before current player; sole survivor leaves first introductory message
Intro cinematic establishes premise: alien-infested planet sent toward Earth long ago; player as transformer aerobot gynoid tasked with infiltration and destruction
Dungeon corridor themes and boss types reflect territorial alien factions

boss_design.philosophy: End-of-corridor encounters marking completion of sealed safety device dungeons; bosses reward power-ups and occasional warp panel keys upon defeat; encounters tied to five hostile alien tribes controlling different Naju territories; boss difficulty scales with player's equipment/upgrade progression

faction_indicators: Five hostile alien tribes competing for territorial control within Naju
Dungeon corridors represent contested faction strongholds
Safety devices linked to territorial control infrastructure
danger_signals: Multiple hostile lifeforms actively defending territories
Failed predecessor guardian(s) evidenced by message panels
Time pressure implicit in 'before it reaches Earth' mission context
environmental_storytelling: Message panels left by previous guardian attempts serve as environmental clues and lore breadcrumbs
Warp panel symbols indicate surrounding territorial areas
Clue rooms within labyrinth contain hints about unsealing locked corridors
Dungeon interior visually distinct from surface labyrinth, representing alien infrastructure
